data.title: Roche collaboration for lead asset boosts Tempest
data.description:
• Shares of Tempest Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TPST) surged ~29% in the pre-market on Thursday after the company announced an agreement with Swiss pharma giant Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) to further develop its lead asset amezalpat (TPST-1120).
  • According to the deal, Roche (OTCQX:RHHBF) will supply its anti-PD-L1 immunotherapy atezolizumab (Tecentriq) to support a Phase 3 trial Tempest (TPST) expects to start in Q1 2025 targeting patients with hepatocellular carcinoma, a form of liver cancer.
  • The pivotal trial is designed to evaluate amezalpat in combination with the current standard of care, atezolizumab plus bevacizumab, as a first-line option for unresectable or metastatic hepatocellular carcinoma.
  • “Based on the positive Phase 2 data, I believe this combination therapy holds the potential to significantly improve first-line liver cancer treatment, and we look forward to amezalpat moving into this pivotal Phase 3 study,” TPST CEO Stephen Brady remarked.

data.title: Vireo Growth names Amber Shimpa as CEO
data.description:
• Vireo Growth (OTCQX:VREOF) said it has appointed Amber Shimpa to the role of CEO, effective immediately.
  • The company added Josh Rosen has stepped down as the CEO, interim CFO, and also from the company's board.
  • Joe Duxbury, who has been with the company since 2019, has been named as the interim CFO.

data.title: Tilray Brands in charts: Cannabis business struggles in FQ1 '25
Immatics stock falls as it looks to raise $150M via public offering
data.description:
• Immatics (NASDAQ:IMTX)  said it has commenced an underwritten public offering of its ordinary shares.
  • The firm plans to raise $150M through the public offering.
  • Shares fell 14.1% in premarket trade.

data.title: IBM in spotlight as RBC ups price target on improving sentiment
data.description:
• IBM (NYSE:IBM) was in the spotlight on Thursday as RBC Capital Markets upped its price target on the IT company, citing improving sentiment.
  • Shares were down fractionally in premarket trading.
  • In addition to rising sentiment, IBM should offer up strong free cash flow figures when it reports third-quarter results later this month, RBC Capital analyst Matthew Swanson wrote to clients. Swanson maintained his Outperform rating on IBM and upped his price target to $250 from $211.
  • In addition, Swanson said there should be greater traction for software sales, especially from Red Hat, which IBM management has said should re-accelerate in the second-half of the year. Performance in the first-half of the year was driven by better-than-expected infrastructure spending, as well as support for generative artificial intelligence.

data.title: Buckle reports fall in comparable store net sales for period October 5
data.description:
• The Buckle (NYSE:BKE) said its comparable store net sales for the 5-week period ended October 5, fell 4.3% Y/Y.
  • The firm added, net sales for the 5-week fiscal month ended October 5, decreased 2.5% to $100.6 million from net sales, as compared to last year.
  • Comparable store net sales year-to-date for the 35-week period ended October 5 were down 6.1% from last year., while net sales for the 35-week fiscal period ended October 5, decreased 5.1% to $750.5 million.

data.title: Tilray sees better days for cannabis amid U.S. presidential race
data.description:
The upcoming U.S. presidential elections will likely bring about positive changes for the cannabis industry, Irwin Simon, CEO and Chairman of Canadian Licensed Producer Tilray Brands (NASDAQ:TLRY), said Thursday.

His remarks coincided with Tilray’s (TLRY) Q1 financials for fiscal 2025, which exceeded Wall Street’s expectations for earnings but missed on revenue.

With both U.S. presidential hopefuls Donald Trump and Kamala Harris pledging support for cannabis reforms, an ongoing review for rescheduling marijuana as a less dangerous compound has gained significance.

“We believe that there is a greater likelihood that the upcoming U.S. presidential elections will result in improved regulatory changes in the cannabis industry, as both candidates have publicly confirmed their support for further legalization,” Simon said.

“We are optimistic about the future of the cannabis industry and look forward to the potential opportunities that lie ahead,” he added.

Meanwhile, Tilray’s (TLRY) Q1 financial figures indicated $200M in net revenue, implying ~ a 13% YoY rise, thanks mainly to the company’s beverage alcohol business, which added $56.0M recording a twofold rise from the year-ago quarter.

However, TLRY’s cannabis and distribution segments contracted, bringing in $61.2M and $68.0M in net revenue and implying a ~13% YoY and ~2% drop, respectively.

However, the company’s net loss fell ~38% YoY to $34.7M as its gross margin improved to 30% compared to 25% in the prior-year quarter.

data.title: Main Street Capital Q3 private loan activity slips vs Q2, rises from a year ago
data.description:
Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) originated or increased commitments in its private loan portfolio of $270.3M and funded total investments across its private loan portfolio with a cost basis totaling $309.3M, the company said on Thursday.

That compares with Q2 private loan portfolio new or increased commitments of $375.9M and funded total investments with a cost basis totaling $323.8M. Still, activity increased from the year-ago quarter, in which new or increased commitments were $107.3M and total funded total investments with a cost basis were $134.6M.

As of Sept. 30, 2024, its private loan portfolio included total investments at a cost of ~$1.9B across 92 unique borrowers vs. ~$1.8B across 92 unique borrowers at the end of Q2. As a percentage of cost, the portfolio included 96.0% invested in first lien debt investments and 4.0% invested in equity investments or other securities.

Among notable new private loan commitments and investments during Q3 2024 are:

  • $77.2M in a first lien senior secured loan and $8.3M in a first lien senior secured revolver to a tech-enabled marketing solutions provider offering services in customer loyalty, channel partner marketing, performance media, website design, and email marketing.
  • $43.6M in a first lien senior secured loan and $1.4M in equity to a provider of maintenance, repair, and overhaul services for industrial equipment.
  • $31.1M in a first lien senior secured loan and $4.9M in a first lien senior secured revolver to an operator of tourism concession and harbor cruises across North America.

data.title: ServiceNow in focus as BMO ups price target on 'durability of growth'
data.description:
• ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) was in focus on Thursday as BMO raised its price target on the cloud computing software company.
  • Shares were little changed in premarket trading.
  • Recent feedback from the value-added reseller community has been positive on ServiceNow, with the pipeline potentially building up, analyst Keith Bachman wrote in a note. Bachman upped his price target to $1,025 and kept his Outperform rating on ServiceNow.
  • In addition, Bachman said it's possible that the Bill McDermott-led ServiceNow can generate "modest" upside to its previous September guidance, which called for 22% year-over-year growth in current remaining performance obligations in constant currency.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Tilray Brands reports mixed FQ1 with 13% growth in revenue and 38% reduction in net loss
